title: Mirror repos/gui gateway-session auth pattern into repos/travel (Round 9, corrected)
description: Replace the now-superseded Cloudflare Access edge-gating plan with the gateway-mediated session auth pattern already used by `repos/gui` (the admin UI behind mestumre.dev). After Auth0 SPA login, travel.mestumre.dev exchanges the Auth0 token at `gateway.mestumre.dev/api/auth/login` for a NoETL session_token, stores it the same way GUI does, and carries it on every playbook execution call. Guest mode is disabled for production builds; preserved for local dev via a Vite env flag. Single identity (kadyapam@gmail.com via Auth0) flows end-to-end. Cap: 1 travel PR + small docs additions. The superseded Cloudflare Access bridge task (`20260513-070000-cloudflare-access-travel.task.json`) stays in place for audit history but is NOT fired. See `memory/inbox/2026/05/20260513-073000-supersede-cloudflare-access-round-9.md`.

pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.status: Minimal. Most of the dependencies already exist from prior rounds.
what_is_ready: Auth0 SPA app for travel.mestumre.dev (callbacks updated, kadyapam@gmail.com authorized; same user as GUI).
Cloudflare Pages deployment of travel at travel.mestumre.dev (live, public for now — this round closes the gap).
gateway.mestumre.dev is the public NoETL endpoint (existing infra; mirrors how GUI talks to it).

NoETL Auth0 login playbook / session endpoint at gateway.mestumre.dev/api/auth/login (already exercised by GUI). Codex inspects GUI to extract the exact request/response shape — DO NOT infer; mirror what GUI does.
GCP secrets `muno-auth0-domain`, `muno-auth0-client-id`, `muno-auth0-audience` from the prior round are still good (the SPA app didn't change, just its callback URLs).
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.what_to_verify_before_firing: Confirm GUI's auth flow actually works end-to-end (Codex re-confirms in phase 1 by examining repos/gui source + maybe a probe). If GUI's auth is broken, this round can't mirror anything from it.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.cloudflare_access_secrets_disposition: The `cloudflare-access-token` GCP secret (from the superseded round) stays — useful for future CF API work. The `cloudflare-access-allow-rule` secret (if you provisioned it) is harmless and can stay or be deleted. Neither is used by this round.

design_brief_for_codex.discovery_phase_in_repos_gui:
The single most important deliverable in phase 1 is a precise map of how `repos/gui` does gateway-session auth. Codex grep + read `repos/gui/src/` for:
  - Anywhere `gateway.mestumre.dev` or `/api/auth/login` or `session_token` or `sessionToken` appears.
  - The Auth0 wrapper (likely `@auth0/auth0-react` if GUI also uses it; or a direct OIDC client if it pre-dates that).
  - The function that exchanges Auth0 token for gateway session_token — capture: endpoint URL, HTTP method, request body shape, request headers (especially how the Auth0 token is delivered), response shape (where session_token lives in the response), what fields the response includes alongside the token (expires_at, refresh_token, user info, etc.).
  - Storage layer: localStorage / sessionStorage / IndexedDB / in-memory zustand store / etc.
  - The HTTP client interceptor that attaches session_token to subsequent gateway calls — capture: header name (likely `Authorization: Bearer <token>` or `X-NoETL-Session: <token>` — don't guess), error handling on 401 (refresh? logout?), token expiry awareness.
  - Logout flow — does GUI invalidate the session at gateway, or just drop the local token?

Produce `repos/travel/docs/auth/gateway-session-pattern.md` documenting the pattern AS IT EXISTS IN GUI before changing anything in travel. This doc is the source of truth for the implementation that follows.
design_brief_for_codex.implementation_in_repos_travel:
Mirror the GUI pattern in `repos/travel/src/`:

  - Update `repos/travel/src/api/noetlClient.ts` (or whatever it's called now after the rename) so the base URL points at `https://gateway.mestumre.dev` in production (configurable via Vite env var `VITE_GATEWAY_BASE_URL` with default).

  - Add `repos/travel/src/api/gatewaySession.ts`:
    - `loginToGateway(auth0Token: string): Promise<{ sessionToken: string, expiresAt?: string, user?: { email, sub, ... } }>` — POSTs to the gateway login endpoint per the pattern doc.
    - `getStoredSession(): { token: string, expiresAt?: string } | null` — reads from the same storage GUI uses.
    - `storeSession(token: string, expiresAt?: string)` — writes via the same mechanism.
    - `clearSession()` — wipes the storage.
    - `withSessionHeader(headers: HeadersInit, token: string): HeadersInit` — adds the auth header in the same format GUI uses.

  - Update `repos/travel/src/App.tsx` (or the auth-aware root component) to:
    1. After Auth0 SPA login completes (existing PR #17 flow), call `getAccessTokenSilently()` to get the Auth0 access token.
    2. Call `loginToGateway(auth0Token)` and store the resulting session_token via `storeSession(...)`.
    3. Surface a 'Linking to gateway...' loading state during the exchange (LoadingCard widget is fine).
    4. On 401 from any gateway call: call `clearSession()` + redirect to Auth0 logout + immediate re-login OR show an error and a Sign in button.

  - Update all places that currently call NoETL directly (the existing noetlClient) so they go through the gateway session — apply the session header from `withSessionHeader`. If there's an axios interceptor in GUI, copy that pattern.

  - DELETE or guard the guest-mode code paths in production builds (see `disable_guest_mode_in_production` below).

  - Update sidebar (in muno's existing Sidebar component — preserving the 'Muno' brand from PR #17): when signed in, show the user's email under their name. When not signed in, the only path is the Sign in button — no Guest mode link.
design_brief_for_codex.disable_guest_mode_in_production:
Guest mode in muno today is the implicit fallback (any user who doesn't log in still gets a working chat thread under `chat_threads/_anonymous-*` Firestore paths). Production behaviour should be: no login = no app.

  - Add Vite env var `VITE_ALLOW_GUEST` (default `false`).
  - In production Cloudflare Pages build, do NOT set this var → guest mode disabled.
  - For local dev: developers can set `VITE_ALLOW_GUEST=true` in their `.env.local` to keep the existing fast iteration loop without needing to spin up the full auth chain.
  - When guest mode is disabled (the default for prod), the App component renders a full-screen Sign in pane when `!isAuthenticated` instead of the chat thread shell. Includes the Adiona logo + a single 'Sign in to start planning' CTA that triggers `loginWithRedirect()`.
  - Document the flag in `repos/travel/docs/auth/auth0-setup.md` 'Guest mode' subsection.
design_brief_for_codex.what_NOT_to_do:
- DO NOT touch Cloudflare Access. The cancelled Round 9 stays cancelled.
  - DO NOT modify Auth0 tenant config (Kadyapam owns Auth0).
  - DO NOT change Cloudflare Pages settings (the CI/CD pipeline keeps deploying on push to main).
  - DO NOT touch DNS records.
  - DO NOT change NoETL's gateway auth endpoint or the Auth0 login playbook in NoETL. This round only changes the travel SPA client side. If gateway needs a fix, that's a separate round.
  - DO NOT add a second Auth0 application — reuse the existing travel SPA app.
  - DO NOT touch `repos/gui`. Read-only inspection only.
  - DO NOT change how Firestore is accessed from the frontend (live listener for calendar_view stays unauthenticated against the permissive v1 rules; tightening that to per-uid is a SEPARATE follow-up round). The session_token gates PLAYBOOK EXECUTION through the gateway, not direct Firestore reads.
  - DO NOT echo any tokens (Auth0 access tokens, gateway session_tokens, refresh tokens) in stdout, logs, memory, or result JSON. Length and prefix (e.g., first 4 chars) only, if at all.
  - DO NOT remove the existing in-app Auth0 SPA flow from PR #17 — extend it. The Auth0 → gateway exchange is layered on top of the existing SPA login.

1_pre_handoff_and_discovery.objective: Verify the prior round's superseding is recorded, then map GUI's gateway auth pattern.
spec: Read `memory/inbox/2026/05/20260513-073000-supersede-cloudflare-access-round-9.md` — confirms the superseding context.
Confirm muno/travel main is at the post-PR-#17 SHA (Codex inspects).
GREP repos/gui for: `gateway.mestumre.dev`, `/api/auth/login`, `session_token`, `sessionToken`, `Bearer`, anything auth-shaped.
READ the relevant GUI files; produce a comprehensive map of the pattern (endpoint, request body shape, response shape, storage mechanism, header attachment, 401 handling, logout flow).

Probe gateway endpoint with the operator's gcloud creds to confirm it's reachable (no actual login — just a `curl -I https://gateway.mestumre.dev/api/auth/login` looking for some response, even if 405 for a GET).
Author `repos/travel/docs/auth/gateway-session-pattern.md` with the full GUI pattern map BEFORE any travel code changes.
If GUI doesn't actually have a gateway auth flow (e.g., it talks to NoETL via a different mechanism): AMBER + STOP. The 'mirror GUI' premise needs Kadyapam's reality check first.
If any verify fails: AMBER + STOP.
1_pre_handoff_and_discovery.verify: Pattern doc exists; the implementation path is grounded in what GUI actually does.

2_extract_shared_pattern.objective: If feasible, factor the auth client into a shared module both GUI and travel can use.
spec: If repos/gui and repos/travel are in the same npm monorepo OR can share via a published package: extract `gatewaySession.ts` into a shared place to avoid drift.
If they are independent repos with no shared layer: just copy the pattern into travel verbatim and document the duplication in the auth docs (with a TODO for future consolidation).
DO NOT spend more than 30 minutes on monorepo restructuring. If sharing requires meaningful refactor, copy + document is the right move.
2_extract_shared_pattern.verify: Decision recorded in the pattern doc; either shared or copied with rationale.

3_implement_travel_auth_flow.objective: Apply the gateway-session pattern inside repos/travel.
spec: Per `design_brief_for_codex.implementation_in_repos_travel`.
Add `src/api/gatewaySession.ts`.
Update `src/api/noetlClient.ts` (or rename to gatewayClient.ts if that matches GUI's naming) to use the gateway base URL + session header.
Update the App root to do the Auth0 → gateway exchange after Auth0 login completes. Show a LoadingCard while exchanging.
Wire 401 handling: invalidate session + redirect to Sign in.
Update locale strings: `auth.linking_gateway`, `auth.session_expired`, `auth.signin_required`, etc.
`npm run type-check`, `npm run test`, `npm run smoke:widgets`, `npm run build` all GREEN.
3_implement_travel_auth_flow.verify: All four npm scripts green; type system reflects the new auth state.

4_disable_guest_mode_in_production.objective: Add VITE_ALLOW_GUEST flag (default false); render a Sign in pane when not authenticated.
spec: Per `design_brief_for_codex.disable_guest_mode_in_production`.
Add `VITE_ALLOW_GUEST` to .env.example with comment.
Update Cloudflare Pages build settings if needed (most likely VITE_ALLOW_GUEST is simply unset in production env, which Vite reads as `undefined` → falsy → guest disabled).
Build the production Sign-in pane component (full-screen, logo, single 'Sign in to start planning' CTA).
Existing chat shell rendering becomes conditional on (isAuthenticated || VITE_ALLOW_GUEST).
4_disable_guest_mode_in_production.verify: `VITE_ALLOW_GUEST=false npm run build && ...preview` shows the Sign-in pane and no chat shell until login. `VITE_ALLOW_GUEST=true npm run dev` keeps the old behaviour.

5_container_build_and_pages_deploy.objective: Build + Cloudflare Pages deploys via the existing CI.
spec: Push the PR — Cloudflare Pages CI builds it automatically on the preview branch.

Verify the preview deploy URL is accessible (typically <branch-name>.<pages-project>.pages.dev) and that the preview build shows the Sign-in pane (since VITE_ALLOW_GUEST is unset in preview by default — verify Pages project settings).
Once merged to main, Cloudflare Pages re-deploys to travel.mestumre.dev.
5_container_build_and_pages_deploy.verify: Preview deploy GREEN; main merge triggers prod deploy.

6_live_smoke_with_kadyapam.objective: Confirm the end-to-end auth flow works against the live gateway.
spec: After main deploys, post a Kadyapam-side smoke checklist in the result JSON (Kadyapam runs in a browser):
1. Open https://travel.mestumre.dev/ in an incognito window.
2. Expect: Sign in pane (no chat shell, no Guest fallback).
3. Click Sign in → complete Auth0 (kadyapam@gmail.com).
4. Expect: brief 'Linking to gateway...' indicator → then chat shell loads.
5. Start a trip ('Find flights to Paris') → confirm widgets render and the agent responds.
6. Open browser devtools → Network tab → verify every gateway request has the session_token Authorization (or whatever header GUI uses).
7. (Optional) Manually invalidate the session token in localStorage → next gateway call should return 401 → app should redirect to Sign in OR auto-re-link.
Codex marks the round AMBER pending Kadyapam's live confirmation; flips to GREEN after Kadyapam confirms steps 1-6 work.

If Kadyapam reports the auth exchange fails: AMBER + leave PR open. Likely the gateway endpoint expects a different request shape than Codex inferred from GUI — pattern doc + actual gateway response in result JSON for next-agent debugging.
6_live_smoke_with_kadyapam.verify: Browser smoke checklist documented; round AMBERs pending Kadyapam confirmation.

7_docs_and_close_out.objective: Memory entry, scoping doc update, ai-meta pointer bumps.
spec: Author `repos/travel/docs/auth/auth0-setup.md` updates: new sections for the gateway exchange flow, the VITE_ALLOW_GUEST flag, the production-vs-local-dev split.
Update `repos/travel/README.md` 'Live' link to note 'Production requires authenticated kadyapam@gmail.com (or allowed user); local dev with VITE_ALLOW_GUEST=true keeps guest mode'.

Update `repos/docs/docs/tutorials/08-trip-planner-end-to-end.md` 'Try it yourself' section to reflect: 'Visiting travel.mestumre.dev now shows a Sign in pane; complete Auth0 to access the planner. Local dev with `VITE_ALLOW_GUEST=true` preserves the prior guest-mode flow.' Replace the now-irrelevant Cloudflare Access mention from the previous tutorial draft if it landed.
Memory entry at `ai-meta/memory/inbox/2026/05/<timestamp>-travel-gateway-session-auth-green.md` (or AMBER if Kadyapam's smoke is pending). Cross-link to the supersede memo.
Update `ai-meta/sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md`: replace the 'Round 9 Cloudflare Access' note with 'Round 9 (corrected): gateway-session auth mirroring GUI'.

Result file: `bridge/outbox/20260513-080000-travel-gateway-session-auth.result.json`. Required: id, status, summary, gui_auth_pattern_doc_path, travel_pr_url, travel_merge_sha, docs_pr_url, ai_meta_submodule_pointer_bump_shas, browser_smoke_checklist (for Kadyapam), kadyapam_smoke_confirmation (filled in once user confirms), cleanup, verdict.
Append a paragraph to `ai-meta/bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md`.
Bump `repos/travel` and `repos/docs` submodule pointers locally. Do NOT push ai-meta.
7_docs_and_close_out.verify: All artefacts written; PRs merged; pattern doc lives in the repo.

architectural_rules: Mirror repos/gui's gateway-session auth pattern verbatim. Inspect-then-implement; do not infer the pattern.
Auth0 SPA flow (PR #17) preserved and extended; the gateway exchange happens AFTER Auth0 login completes.
Guest mode disabled by default in production. VITE_ALLOW_GUEST=true preserves it for local dev.
No Cloudflare Access. No edge gating. The cancelled Round 9 stays cancelled per the supersede memo.
No Auth0 tenant config changes. No DNS changes. No Cloudflare Pages settings changes (other than maybe ensuring VITE_ALLOW_GUEST is unset for prod).
Single identity (kadyapam@gmail.com via Auth0) flows through Auth0 → gateway login → session_token → playbook execution.
No new GCP secrets (the existing Auth0 secrets cover everything needed).
Read-only inspection of repos/gui; do NOT modify gui in this round.
Frontend Firestore live reads (calendar_view live mode) stay unauthenticated against v1 permissive rules. Tightening Firestore-side auth is a separate follow-up round.
Never echo Auth0 access tokens, gateway session_tokens, or refresh tokens in stdout / logs / memory / result JSON. Length + first-4-chars only if absolutely needed.
If gateway exchange fails during Kadyapam's smoke: AMBER + leave PR open with the pattern doc + actual gateway error response captured for next-agent debugging.
PR via standard flow on noetl/travel main.
ai-meta pointer bumps local-only; Kadyapam pushes ai-meta.
result_file_instructions: Write `bridge/outbox/20260513-080000-travel-gateway-session-auth.result.json`. Required: id, status (GREEN once Kadyapam confirms browser smoke; AMBER pending is a valid intermediate state), summary, gui_pattern_summary {endpoint, method, request_shape, response_shape_fields_only, storage_mechanism, session_header_name}, travel_pr_url, travel_merge_sha, docs_pr_url, ai_meta_submodule_pointer_bump_shas, vite_allow_guest_flag_added (bool), guest_mode_disabled_in_prod_build (bool), kadyapam_smoke_checklist, kadyapam_smoke_confirmation (initially empty; user fills in via a follow-up commit when complete), cleanup, verdict.
references: repos/gui/ (SOURCE OF TRUTH for the pattern to mirror — read-only)
noetl/travel#17 (in-app Auth0 SPA — the foundation this round extends)
https://travel.mestumre.dev/ (live deployment; public until this round lands)
https://gateway.mestumre.dev/ (the NoETL gateway; auth endpoint discovered in phase 1)
